evidence_lines: #1 Agent to Agent Testing Platform
Deploy autonomous AI evaluators to test your chatbots, voice assistants, and calling agents for hallucinations, bias, toxicity, compliance, and more.
An AI Agent for Testing AI Agents
AI agents don't produce the same output twice. Agent-to-agent testing deploys an AI evaluator that engages your agent like a real user, scoring every response for accuracy, safety, and compliance.
Detect hallucinations and fabricated claims automatically.
Uncover bias across demographics and personas.
Screen for toxicity and compliance violations.
Every Agent Type. One Platform.
Phone Caller Inbound Agent
Phone Caller Outbound Agent
Chat & Voice Agent Testing
Score every conversation across 9 quality metrics — from hallucination and bias detection to context awareness and conversation flow.
Score bias, hallucination, completeness, context awareness, response quality, and more.
Auto-generate 60-100+ test scenarios from uploaded docs, PRDs, or connected JIRA and Confluence.
Test IVR systems and inbound support bots with real phone calls and score across 30+ metrics.
Track real-time call duration, speaker-identified transcripts, and DTMF detection.
Measure FCR, intent recognition, CSAT, containment rate, voice quality, and STT accuracy.
Production Recording Analysis
Upload production call recordings for batch analysis with the same evaluation metrics.
Evaluate outbound sales dialers and proactive outreach agents with dedicated number pooling and passive monitoring.
Reserve dedicated outbound number pools with country code selection for realistic testing.
Monitor live outbound agent calls for QA without disrupting the conversation.
Configure voice selection, response timing, and 15 background noise presets.
Score AI-generated images against prompts, technical specs, and brand guidelines with batch analysis of up to 50 images.
Overall image-prompt alignment score with match and discrepancy tracking as Pass, Fail, or Partial.
Batch Analysis & Analytics
Analyze up to 50 images per run with 30-day quality trends and prompt performance rankings.
Autonomous Testing for Every Agent You Build
Calculate based on evaluation volume, giving you a reliable signal on whether your AI agent's quality scores are ready to act upon.

records.reason: Background article on AI-agent evaluation framing.
records.title: AI Agent Evaluation: What Most Teams Miss [2026] | TestMu AI (Formerly LambdaTest)
records.description: AI agent evaluation covers the metrics, methods, and tools teams need to measure task completion, tool accuracy, and safety adherence.
headings: AI Agent Evaluation: What Most Teams Miss [2026]
What Are the Benefits of AI Agent Evaluation
How to Evaluate an AI Agent
How to Build Evaluation-Driven Development Into Agent Workflow
What Are the Key AI Agent Evaluation Metrics
What Are the Types of AI Agent Evaluation
Best AI Agent Evaluation Tools in 2026
1. TestMu AI Agent to Agent Testing
AI Agent Evaluation vs Manual AI Agent Testing
evidence_lines: AI Agent Evaluation: What Most Teams Miss [2026] | TestMu AI (Formerly LambdaTest)
AI Agent Evaluation: What Most Teams Miss [2026]
AI agent evaluation covers the metrics, methods, and tools teams need to measure task completion, tool accuracy, and safety adherence.
What Is AI Agent Evaluation
Benefits of AI Agent Evaluation
Types of AI Agent Evaluation
Best AI Agent Evaluation Tools
How Do You Evaluate an AI Agent
Define objectives, build failure-based test datasets, instrument execution traces, score reasoning and action layers separately, and monitor behavioral drift continuously.
: Specify correct agent behavior per task type before writing test cases. Two domain experts should reach the same pass/fail verdict independently. If they cannot, the task needs refinement.
: Start with 20 to 50 tasks drawn from real failures, not invented scenarios. Waiting too long means reverse-engineering success criteria from a live system.
: Capture every tool call, argument, and decision per run. The final output tells you something failed. The trace tells you where and why.
Score Both Layers Separately
: Set baselines before deployment and compare live traffic continuously. Agents degrade without anyone touching them because the environment around them changes.
Which AI Agent Evaluation Tools Are Worth Using in 2026
These are the AI agent evaluation tools teams are actively using in production for tracing, scoring, and pre-deployment scenario testing.
: It is a unified platform to validate chatbots, voice bots, and phone agents using 15+ specialized AI testing agents across thousands of real-world scenarios.
: It is a Python-first, pytest-style framework with component-level metrics for reasoning accuracy and tool call correctness, without requiring a custom evaluation harness.
: It is an end-to-end evaluation and observability with built-in simulation. Best suited for running large-scale pre-deployment scenario testing.
AI agent evaluation measures how reliably an autonomous AI agent completes tasks, selects tools, and sequences actions across its full execution path.
LangChain State of Agent Engineering report
What Are the Benefits of AI Agent Evaluation
AI agent evaluation reduces deployment risk, catches silent failures before users encounter them, and gives teams the baseline needed to improve agents systematically.
: Invalid tool invocations, malformed parameters, and memory retrieval errors never surface in a text quality score. Trace-level evaluation catches them before users do.
: Component-level scoring tells you whether the failure was in the LLM's planning logic or a tool call with an invalid parameter, turning a multi-hour debug into a precise fix.
: An agent taking twelve tool calls to finish a three-step task is an infrastructure cost problem at scale. Tracking average tool calls per task is both a quality and a cost signal.
Validate your AI agents with 15+ specialized testing agents.
How to Evaluate an AI Agent
Define correct behavior per task type, build test datasets from real failures, score both execution layers independently, and monitor production continuously.
sits beneath agent evaluation as the foundational layer, covering model accuracy, bias, and drift before agents are wired together into multi-step pipelines.

TestMu AI Unveils the World’s First True AI Agent-to-Agent Testing Platform!
TestMu AI's Agent-to-Agent Testing Platform is the world's first solution for testing AI agents using specialized AI agents, boosting test coverage and ensuring flawless AI performance.
But Why Do We Need AI to Test AI Agents?
Introducing the World’s First Agentic Testing Platform
Automated Multi-Agent Test Generation
Comprehensive Test Scenarios
Seamless Integration with HyperExecute
“Over 80% of enterprises now deploy AI agents in production, yet most lack adequate testing frameworks for these intelligent systems.”
The uncomfortable truth? No one knows how to properly test these AI agents.
These challenges underscore the need for structured approaches to
— from functional and performance validation to bias detection and accuracy benchmarking.
Ship flawless AI agents with Agent to Agent Testing Platform!
Generates thousands of diverse test scenarios automatically
Adapts to your agent’s behavior patterns
such as text, voice, and hybrid deployments with equal sophistication
Your requirements don’t live in a single format, and neither should your testing. Go beyond text! Our agent-to-agent testing platform easily understands context from:

Smart testing agents automatically explore, test, and validate your AI systems by running thousands of different scenarios and challenges, providing thorough testing that grows with your AI’s complexity.
and GenAI ensure more varied, expert-driven test cases compared to a single general-purpose agent.

Generate comprehensive test scenarios across multiple categories, ensuring thorough validation of your conversational AI systems and applications. Test scenarios are auto-generated across different categories such as:
Our testing agents learn and adjust their testing methods to match how your AI behaves in real situations, giving you complete test coverage that traditional testing methods simply cannot provide.
We’ve tightly integrated our agent-to-agent testing platform with
’s HyperExecute infrastructure for massively parallel cloud execution. Once test scenarios are generated:
Generate test scenarios and run them at scale with minimal setup, delivering actionable feedback faster than ever before.

Assess test results with customizable response schemas or sample outputs for clear, categorized insights. Make data-driven decisions on agent performance and optimization by evaluating key metrics like:
Bias detection and mitigation

Ensure relevance, accuracy, and efficiency across all your AI agent deployments with comprehensive performance analytics.

records.title: What Are AI Agents? Components, Types and Examples
records.description: Explore AI agents - their benefits, core components, how they work, different types, and common challenges with real-world examples.
headings: What Are AI Agents? Components, Types and Examples
What Are Some Real-World Applications of AI Agents?
How to Use AI Agents: Example Using TestMu AI KaneAI
AI Agents vs AI Assistants vs AI Bots: Key Differences
evidence_lines: What Are AI Agents? Components, Types and Examples
Explore AI agents - their benefits, core components, how they work, different types, and common challenges with real-world examples.
Core Components of AI Agents
What Are Some Real-World Applications of AI Agents?
How to Use AI Agents: Example Using TestMu AI KaneAI
Challenges With Using AI Agents
AI Agents vs AI Assistants vs AI Bots: Key Differences
AI agents are autonomous software systems or programs that can perceive their environment, make decisions, and take actions to achieve specific goals.
Define a clear objective for the AI agent to complete.
Continuously improve the agent's performance by adjusting strategies based on feedback.
AI agents offer some key benefits that go beyond simple automation. Here’s how they’re reshaping customer interactions and business processes:

This component uses the data from Perception and Memory to make decisions and plan actions. It analyzes the information, considers possible results, and determines the most effective way to achieve the agent's goals.
An AI agent can perform actions autonomously, typically in response to a goal or task defined by a user or the system itself.
The process starts when you set a clear goal for the AI agent. This can be a specific task, a question, or a goal to be accomplished.
The AI agent understands the environment to complete the task. This involves collecting relevant data. The input can come from various sources like the user, external systems, sensors, or APIs.

The agent uses AI techniques such as Natural Language Processing (NLP) to interpret your request. It doesn’t just take the words literally, but understands the context and the underlying goal or objective behind them.
With a plan in place, the AI agent carries out the task. It follows the predefined steps to complete the goal, performing the required actions without further human intervention.
After performing the action, the agent waits for feedback to evaluate the success of the task. It learns from this feedback, whether it’s positive or negative, and uses it to improve future actions.

With continuous feedback, the AI agent refines its processes, learning from past experiences to improve its performance in future tasks. It continuously adjusts its decision-making to be more effective and efficient.

Over time, the agent may learn to prioritize certain types of checks (like database migrations) during deployment, or it may adapt to handle issues faster by rolling back a deployment more quickly when issues arise.
Let’s break down AI agents based on their types. Each type represents a different level of complexity and is suited to different types of tasks.
Model-Based Reflex Agents:
Multi-Agent Systems (MAS):
AI agents are being used in many areas to automate work, optimize processes, and improve decision-making.
Voice assistants and intelligent
How to Use AI Agents: Example Using

- a GenAI-native testing agent built on modern Large Language Models. You can use it to plan, author, and evolve tests using natural language, simplifying your testing process and making it more efficient.
Automatically detects and fixes broken locators during test execution, reducing maintenance efforts and enhancing test reliability.
Though AI agents offer benefits in terms of automation, effectiveness, and decision-making, they are used alongside a set of challenges. They are as follows:
AI agents have difficulty handling tasks that require understanding emotions or making complex moral decisions. AI models can sometimes produce biased or unfair results.

records.title: Kane CLI Skills for AI Agents | TestMu AI (Formerly LambdaTest)
records.description: Install the Kane CLI skill for Claude Code, Codex CLI, or Gemini CLI to enable browser automation directly from your AI coding agent.
headings: Kane CLI Skills for AI Agents
Test across 3000+ combinations of browsers, real devices & OS.
evidence_lines: Kane CLI Skills for AI Agents
is a markdown instruction file that teaches an AI coding agent how to use
flag). The skill tells the agent to always use
The quickest way to install the Kane CLI skill globally for all supported agents (Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI) in one command:
npx @testmuai/kane-cli-skill
If you prefer to install manually for a specific agent, follow the instructions below.
The Kane CLI skill for Claude Code is a
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/kane-cli
curl -o ~/.claude/skills/kane-cli/SKILL.md \
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/LambdaTest/kane-cli/main/skill-installer/skills/SKILL.md
mkdir -p .claude/skills/kane-cli
curl -o .claude/skills/kane-cli/SKILL.md \
After installing, Claude Code automatically loads the skill. No restart required.
Run multiple browser tests in parallel using sub-agents
Suggest bug reports for agent failures, linked to the GitHub repo
The Kane CLI skill for Codex CLI is appended to your
curl -o /tmp/kane-cli-agents.md \
# Append to global AGENTS.md (creates the file if it doesn't exist)
cat /tmp/kane-cli-agents.md >> ~/.codex/AGENTS.md
cat /tmp/kane-cli-agents.md >> AGENTS.md
The Kane CLI skill for Gemini CLI is a
mkdir -p ~/.gemini/skills/kane-cli
curl -o ~/.gemini/skills/kane-cli/SKILL.md \
mkdir -p .gemini/skills/kane-cli
curl -o .gemini/skills/kane-cli/SKILL.md \
First-Time Auth in Agent Contexts
OAuth login opens a browser window: AI agents cannot complete this flow. Use Basic Auth instead:
When you ask your AI agent to test something on the web, the skill:
kane-cli run --agent --headless
Rates the run and suggests a bug report for low-quality agent failures

records.description: Write browser tests as Markdown files that replay from cache after the first run: no LLM cost, faster execution. Commit tests to git, share recordings, and run in CI.
headings: When to Use testmd vs run ​
Recording a Test from a Live Session ​
Test across 3000+ combinations of browsers, real devices & OS.
evidence_lines: ) and commit them to your repo. On the first run, the AI agent authors each step and saves a recording. On every subsequent run, each step
with no LLM cost and much faster execution. Commit the test file and its recordings to git so teammates and CI can re-run the same tests without re-authoring.
kane-cli testmd run amazon_test.md --agent
On the first run, the agent authors each step and caches the recording. On every later run, the steps replay from cache instantly.
is one-shot. It runs an objective, uploads results, and exits. It is ideal for quick, one-off verifications like checking if a page loads correctly or extracting a value from a live site.
Recording a Test from a Live Session
Max agent reasoning steps per step. Default:
Inline Markdown or file path for agent context
title for the test. This is purely decorative. Kane CLI ignores everything before the first
heading marks a test step. The heading text is a label for your reference; the agent reads the
underneath it. Write the body as either plain English prose describing what the agent should do, or a single
Click submit and verify the confirmation banner.
tells Kane CLI that a failure on this step should not fail the overall test. The run continues to the next step.
After the first run, each step replays from its cached recording with no AI agent, no LLM cost, and much faster execution. A step replays only if
A recording for that step exists
The step's prose is unchanged since the recording
Edit only that step (steps after it cascade automatically)
Variables and context propagate into helpers automatically
kane-cli run "Search for noise-cancelling headphones on amazon.com" --name amazon-search
On exit, Kane CLI writes the test file to
kane-cli testmd run <path> --agent
kane-cli testmd status <path>
kane-cli testmd export <path>
Regenerate code export from existing recordings (no browser launch)
kane-cli testmd delete <path>
cache locally. Does NOT delete from Test Manager
(replay-only, no upload),

.internal/ # Cached recordings, do not edit
. Commit it to git so teammates and CI replay the same recordings.

records.title: Fetch Scenario details associated with your Job ID — TestMu AI API | TestMu AI (Formerly LambdaTest)
records.description: Manage and organize your test builds, test sessions, tunnel status and more with TestMu AI APIs.
headings: Fetch Scenario details associated with your Job ID
evidence_lines: Fetch Scenario details associated with your Job ID
This API retrieves scenario-level execution details for a given Job ID. A scenario in HyperExecute represents a logical stage or group of test cases executed within a [
](https://www.lambdatest.com/support/docs/hyperexecute-guided-walkthrough/#jobs-page). Each job consists of multiple tasks, and each task may contain one or more scenarios.
https://api.hyperexecute.cloud
"https://api.hyperexecute.cloud/v2.0/job/{jobID}/scenarios"
Limits the number of scenarios shown in the response (default is 10, max is 20).
Helps in moving across pagination. Accepts an "id" value and returns scenarios with an "id" greater than or equal to it.
Filter scenarios based on their execution status.
